author: __flcl
content: <@954738828445372416> when receipts are requested and some blocks are empty, do you guys just omit empty receipts or return [] for such block hash

author: taratorio
content:
for empty blocks (i assume by empty blocks you mean blocks with no transactions) we return []
for the `"using b.HeaderNoCopy().ReceiptHash instead of rawdb.ReadHeaderByHash(db, hash) makes it pass"` - that's a bit unexpected
do you have an example request you can give me? i can try to reproduce and check what is happening

author: __flcl
content: try to query eth/70 receipts with such hashes: ["0x80a9b4eb1729e105e0da9fd23963a3d62338c4912cca5735b7e4cc0439e2ceeb","0xdbfcb228b8dbe21baf9e7ac28883a9d1db313f4731fddc91055972fa6b934293","0x911fdd877e74c0fb756087fb20c716c0877489c2562d534e336c9af8b00a6772","0x37f311f6b90b1d37ae1a6b95bd74bb98b92526cc558c78c35f4282beedd7f38e","0x567a01d07c480c3f59e6afd1fb7c27f349b1476c9f65e2e67f194affa4305de8","0x3402a45a3366854d8db7bb9ad2884aa6aa57c6d6b0dfc59626e05c344def6bea","0xd19aca0e51e1d6b98a863d7fbb42b02fd287734f07f6916f1efb53a2ecf6b1f2","0xaf29ee2ae9f712c1c83461dd4a7f93e5ed7923c3b5f5056fc2128c7a67a9ccc6"]

author: __flcl
content: Here an ai test placed at p2p\protocols\eth

author: taratorio
content:
thanks, i think i know what may be happening.
just to confirm you guys kick us out now because of this?

author: __flcl
content: Yes, neth compares receipt count and tx count for a block and bc of the shift it thinks receipt count > tx count, raise and exception and should disconnect

author: taratorio
content: ok, makes sense. this is a good finding

author: taratorio
content: ill add a fix in a bit

author: __flcl
content: Pushed nethermind bal sync fix. Nice thread btw! Sparse blobs testing could be next thing (hope to place it into the branch tomorrow)
